name: Akin
quote: A gold-leaf-laden and heritage-driven restaurant in Old Toronto merging molecular gastronomy with a three-hour food tour of Asia. Chef Eric Chong was also the recipient of the Michelin Young Chef Award.

name: Arbequina
quote: Named after a Spanish olive, this Mediterranean oasis in Roncesvalles serves up Halal, zero-proof cuisine with soul. Fresh Ontario produce shines in mezze-style dishes rooted in Middle Eastern tradition. With a warm welcome and vibrant, non-alcoholic mocktails, Arbequina invites you to linger.

name: Sammarco
signature_dish: dry-aged cuts with Sicilian herbs or lemon oil
quote: A refined Italian steakhouse that's stripped of gimmicks and full of confidence. Only four dry-aged cuts appear on the menu, each grilled simply and served with Sicilian herbs or lemon oil. It's intimate, elegant, and ideal for red wine and conversation.

name: Bar Eugenie
cuisine: European small plates / natural wine bar
quote: A compact, 35-seat restaurant run by Alo alumni, where the menu changes daily and the energy feels spontaneous. Creative small plates — from seasonal crudos to smoky breads — pair beautifully with natural wines. It's elegant yet relaxed, with a loyal local following already forming.

name: Golden Horseshoe Barbecue
quote: A line already forms before noon for this Texas-style smokehouse, where everything from brisket to ribs to sausages is smoked overnight over oak and hickory. The vibe is casual, the trays are heavy, and the scent of slow-cooked meat carries down the block.

name: Liliana
signature_dish: handmade pasta with chili oil and XO sauce
quote: Chef Marvin Palomo merges Italian fundamentals with Asian inspiration in this understated gem. Handmade pastas meet spicy chili oil and XO sauce, while the intimate setting invites lingering. Liliana is soulful, inventive, and effortlessly current.

name: N.L. Ginzburg
quote: Part trattoria, part bistro, N.L. Ginzburg blends Italian and Jewish culinary traditions into soulful, home-style dishes. Expect rustic pasta, grilled meats, and vibrant vegetable plates served in a cozy room that hums with warmth and history.

name: Oro Luxury Dining
quote: Theatrical and opulent, Oro serves global tasting menus presented as 'episodes.' Expect playful fusion — butter chicken pizza beside lobster rolls — and a room bathed in moody gold light. Dinner here feels like a performance worth dressing up for.

name: The Frederick
quote: Cory Vitiello returns downtown with a menu that bridges refined business lunch and lively after-work bar. House-made pastas, classic burgers, and smart cocktails define a space that's both polished and welcoming — a rare balance in the core.

name: TONO
quote: Features Nikkei cuisine — the meeting of Japanese and Peruvian flavors — at the W Hotel rooftop on Bloor Street East.

name: Ayla
quote: Ayla is a hidden gem perched above Dundas West, blending Toronto energy with nostalgic Cantonese touches. With its moody lighting and a menu that zigzags between East and West, Ayla makes every plate feel like a cross-cultural love letter.

name: Belle Isle
quote: From the team behind Lake Inez comes this joyful Little India newcomer. Belle Isle rewrites the rules of dining with quirky snacks, inventive drinks, and an atmosphere that hums with personality. Each visit feels spontaneous — like an impromptu dinner party with wildly talented friends.

name: Bonito's
quote: Don't be fooled by the laundromat façade — Bonito's is a playful mashup of Ecuadorian and Korean comfort food tucked into a diner-bar setting at 180 Ossington Ave. The menu is as bold as it is unexpected, full of spicy, tangy, and delightfully cheeky creations.

name: Casa Morales
quote: This modern cantina brings Mexican soul to Kensington Market with a refined edge. Think elevated street food meets Spanish-style tapas, wrapped in a lively atmosphere and lifted by an outstanding cocktail program. Casa Morales is made for lingering — and ordering one more round.

name: Dopamina
quote: Designed for delight, Dopamina is a sensory playground of bold cocktails and inventive plates. The menu dances between Asian and Mediterranean flavours, with atmospheric lighting and science-lab drinks that feel almost alchemical.

name: Harlem
quote: Soul food establishment blending soul food, art, and live music on Queen West at 745 Queen Street West, featuring catfish and jazz offerings.

name: louf
quote: Opened by Chef Fadi Kattan near Casa Loma, louf offers a globe-trotting tribute to Palestinian cuisine. Ancient recipes meet modern sensibility, with sustainable sourcing and a flair for presentation that turns each plate into a story. Don't miss the labaneh or sumac-spiced dishes, best enjoyed on the patio under a summer sky.

name: Lunch Lady
quote: Vietnamese street food spot bringing Saigon soul with phở, bánh mì, and night-market ambiance to 93 Ossington Ave. Made legendary by Anthony Bourdain, Ms. Nguyen Thi Thanh's cooking translates from Saigon street stall to Toronto with the same bold, humble spirit.

name: Occhiolino
quote: This Italian gem in the Annex invites you to slow down and savour. With hand-made pasta, dreamy interiors, and an ambiance that feels lifted from the pages of a design magazine, Occhiolino is where craftsmanship meets comfort. Their tagliatelle, paired with a glass of something sparkling, makes for a perfect escape.

name: Tutto Panino
quote: Born from a pop-up and the musical minds of Charlotte Day Wilson and friends, Tutto Panino is now a Roncesvalles fixture. This sandwich shop hits every note with perfectly stacked Italian panini, creamy cannoli, and a vibe that blends retro charm with downtown cool.

name: Zia's Place
quote: Co-founded by Jess Maiorano, Zia's Place features handmade pasta and Sunday-supper warmth with Southern Italian flavour at 1543 Dundas Street West.

name: Linny's
quote: Added to the Michelin-recommended list in 2025. Linny's is a casual sandwich shop at 174 Ossington serving thick-cut pastrami and house-made hot sauce.

name: Ricky + Olivia
quote: A chef duo in Leslieville known for '90s-inspired snacks and playful menus that highlight Ontario's bounty.

name: Aera
quote: Oliver & Bonacini's crown jewel on the 38th floor of The Well offers unmatched city views with elevated classics and steakhouse favourites, a sushi counter, and seasonal raw bar delicacies, a curated selection of steak and chops, and sophisticated spins on classic comfort food.

name: Animl Steakhouse and Cocktail Den
quote: This INK Entertainment steakhouse opened fall 2024 in a King West laneway with design by Nivek Remas. The intimate and sophisticated vibe features modern interpretations of classic steakhouse favourites, from dry-aged steaks and fresh seafood to indulgent sides.

name: Bar Prima
signature_dish: lobster fra diavola, octopus alla griglia, striploin pepe verde
quote: Named one of Air Canada's Best New Restaurants of 2024, Bar Prima at 1136 Queen West offers comforting old-school Italian fare from chef-owners Craig Harding and Julian D'Ippolito. Standout dishes include lobster fra diavola, octopus alla griglia, and striploin pepe verde.

name: Blue Bovine
quote: Co-owned by Kevin O'Leary and Toronto Maple Leafs' Mitch Marner, this Liberty Group steakhouse opened January 2024 at the northeast corner of Union Station. Offerings include an extensive steak list, seafood and sushi towers, caviar, and jam-packed salads.

name: DaNico
quote: Liberty Entertainment Group and Michelin Star Chef Daniele Corona transformed a historic bank building at 440 College Street into this modern Italian restaurant. The spot's signature tasting menu highlights innovative cuisine, with artwork from Mr. Brainwash featured throughout.

name: Daphne
quote: This INK Entertainment restaurant at 67 Richmond Street West offers modern American fare with conversation-worthy design details like its dramatic arched ceilings. The menu features wood-grilled meats and seafood, pizzas, pastas, and a dreamy truffle baked potato.

name: Estiatorio Milos Toronto
quote: This high-end global chain opened September 2024 and serves elevated Greek fare with their fresher-than-fresh fish the star of the show, from sleek, sprawling quarters at 330 Bay Street. The restaurant hosted exclusive pre-opening TIFF parties.

name: Frenchy Bar et Brasserie
signature_dish: French onion soup, escargots, truffle baked brie
quote: Opening early 2025, Frenchy Bar et Brasserie provides a mini escape to Paris with a distinct Parisian-inspired vibe. The menu includes French onion soup, escargots, truffle baked brie, coquilles St. Jacques, and salmon tartare, plus Thursday live bands and weekend DJ sets.

name: Mott 32
signature_dish: homemade dim sum, 42-day apple wood roasted Peking duck
quote: This award-winning Chinese restaurant at 190 University Avenue, replacing Momofuku in summer 2024, is best known for their homemade dim sum, their fine Kobe and wagyu beef, and their famed 42-day apple wood roasted Peking duck.

name: Nobu Toronto
quote: Known for its renowned Nobu-style Japanese cuisine with South American influences and its sophisticated vibe. The two-storey restaurant sits at the base of what will become the highly anticipated Nobu Hotel at 25 Mercer Street.

name: The Berczy Tavern
quote: Opening summer 2024, this piano bar at 69 Front Street East occupies a cosy historic building with brick and exposed beams. Diners enjoy live piano accompaniment with dishes including truffle risotto, fish crudo, braised shortrib, and charred octopus.

name: Alma
quote: Ranked #75 in Canada's 100 Best Restaurants 2025. Brings a burst of light to Yorkville with its vibrant, contemporary take on traditional Lebanese cuisine.

name: Bernhardt's
quote: Ranked #45 in Canada's 100 Best Restaurants 2025 at 202 Dovercourt Road, described as a cozy neighbourhood spot known for chicken and incredible vegetables.

name: Osteria Giulia
quote: Ranked #72 in Canada's 100 Best Restaurants 2025. Michelin-starred Italian restaurant that is the predecessor to Giulietta and Sammarco.

name: Quetzal
quote: Ranked #8 in Canada's 100 Best Restaurants 2025. Michelin-starred upscale Mexican wood-fire restaurant with house-made salsas showcasing a variety of chillies.

name: Takja BBQ
quote: Ranked #68 in Canada's 100 Best Restaurants 2025. Sleek Korean BBQ where staff grill for you tableside with dry-aged meats and fresh seafood.
